classification_rationale: Self-declared research/tooling repo for economic simulation, NOT production or protocol logic. README.md:9 "Status: Research-Grade / Placeholder Models"; README.md:14 "Economic models are PLACEHOLDER / toy implementations"; README.md:24-26 "Do NOT use for: Production parameter decisions... Whitepaper claims... Smart contract configuration". .droid/manifest.json:13 "This is a research/tooling repo, not production logic". PR template (.github/pull_request_template.md:9-13) and feature_request.md:21 explicitly gate out DAG/scoring/engine/contract/UI code, confirming the repo is scoped to research/simulation only. package.json:5 private:true. No normative schemas, no contracts, no USS/CPJ/TSSD/Mongo/on-chain code present.
primary_purpose: A dual-stack (TypeScript + Python) deterministic, config-driven economic simulation toolkit that models AFI token emissions, epoch-pulse rhythm, role-based reward/gauge allocation, and payouts for research, parameter-sensitivity, and whitepaper-chart purposes -- explicitly placeholder/toy formulas pending afi-math integration.
protocol_touchpoints: mint/receipt (consumes receipts.json as simulation input, models tokensMinted/cumulativeSupply)
DAG/pipeline (econ pipeline stages: gauge->safety->payouts, NOT scoring DAG)
replay/determinism (seeded RNG, provenance stamps, econ replay command, determinism tests)
registries/reputation (consumes BenchKit merit scores: reputation/poi/poinsight to weight allocation)
schemas (local Pydantic + Zod sim-config schemas only, non-normative)
SDK/API (CLI tools: TS afi-econ + Python afi-econ-kit)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: TypeScript models in `src/` are the **canonical implementations**
bench_data = _load_benchkit_scores(getattr(args, 'scores', None))  # gauge allocation consumes afi-benchkit scores as the merit source
benchkit_repo = find_sibling_repo('afi-benchkit')  # audit assumes a sibling afi-benchkit checkout produces scores.json

contradictions.title: Repo models multi-role econ splits (gauge) while reference mint path is single-beneficiary
contradictions.detail: This research kit models allocation across producers/enrichment/validators/public_goods with per-role caps, AIM pool, maturity holdbacks, and BenchKit merit blending -- the 'econ splits/gauge' direction (KNOWN TENSION #4). It diverges from the current single-beneficiary MintCoordinated receipt reality. Not a defect of THIS repo (it is explicitly research and out of the production path), but it is the concrete locus where the splits-vs-single-beneficiary tension is being explored; nothing here is yet reflected in normative receipt/contract specs.
contradictions.evidence: params/gauge_v0.yaml:4-11 'weights: producers: 0.55 enrichment: 0.25 validators: 0.10 public_goods: 0.10 / caps: per_role_max: 0.60'; src/afi_econ_kit/payouts.py:130 'def compute_epoch_payouts(... gauge_shares, role_credits, maturity_hold_frac)'

contradictions.title: Reputation/merit (BenchKit) blended directly into allocation weights
contradictions.detail: allocation_gauge applies BenchKit merit multipliers (reputation/poi/poinsight) to per-role allocation. North Star permits reputation to influence SELECTION & ALLOCATION (allowed here) but it MUST NOT override deterministic scoring/finality. This repo only affects allocation of an emissions pool and does not touch scoring -- consistent with the rule, but worth flagging as the place to keep that boundary explicit if formulas become canonical.
contradictions.evidence: src/afi_econ_kit/gauge.py:159-175 'if bench_merit and bench_merit_weights: ... raw_allocation[role] *= multipliers[role]'

contradictions.title: Local determinism/provenance mechanism parallel to VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC
contradictions.detail: Repo defines its own provenance stamp (git_sha, config_hash, data_hash, seed) and determinism guarantees independent of the protocol VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC / replay-session model. Acceptable for a research kit, but if any formula is promoted to canonical, its replay artifacts must be reconciled with the normative replay spec rather than this ad-hoc stamp.
contradictions.evidence: src/afi_econ_kit/schemas.py:97-104 'class ProvenanceStamp(BaseModel): version... git_sha... config_hash... data_hash'; tests/test_replay_determinism.py:222 'required_fields = ["timestamp", "git_short_hash", "seed", "data_hash"]'

contradictions.title: out_audit/AUDIT.md asserts 'WHITEPAPER READY' while README says models are placeholders
contradictions.detail: The committed audit report concludes the econ system is mathematically sound and 'WHITEPAPER READY', whereas README.md:24-26 explicitly says do NOT use for whitepaper claims (formulas not final). The audit validates conservation/determinism invariants of the harness, not formula correctness, so the 'whitepaper ready' framing can mislead readers into treating toy formulas as canonical.
contradictions.evidence: out_audit/AUDIT.md:29 'WHITEPAPER READY: All critical checks pass...suitable for academic publication.' vs README.md:25 'Whitepaper claims (formulas are not final)'

upstream: afi-benchkit (scores.json: reputation/poi/poinsight consumed by gauge; scripts/end_to_end_audit.py:147 find_sibling_repo('afi-benchkit'))
afi-math (intended future source of real curves/decay; README.md:16,61 - not yet wired)
downstream: afi-token (econ models intended to INFORM contract parameters; README.md:58 - advisory only, no code dependency)
afi-core / afi-reactor (named as conceptual relations only; README.md:59-60 - no import/runtime dependency)

recommended_action: tag stale (rename internal afi-econ-kit identifiers + remove hardcoded path) and clarify reference-vs-normative (gate the 'WHITEPAPER READY' audit claim against the placeholder-model warning)

notable_findings.title: No Mongo / USS / CPJ / TSSD / vault / on-chain / BigQuery references anywhere
notable_findings.evidence: grep for mongodb|MongoTSSD|vault|TSSD|VaultedSignalRecord, usignal|USS|cpj|CPJ, BigQuery|kafka|warehouse, postgresql|timescaledb|influxdb across all .ts/.py/.md/.json/.yaml returned zero hits (only non-protocol substring matches excluded).
notable_findings.recommendation: None. Confirms repo holds no evidence/commitment/analytics storage layer and imposes no stack mandate -- it is purely a sim harness.

notable_findings.title: Repo models multi-role econ splits + caps + AIM pool + maturity holdbacks
notable_findings.evidence: params/gauge_v0.yaml:4-11 'producers: 0.55 enrichment: 0.25 validators: 0.10 public_goods: 0.10 / caps: per_role_max: 0.60'; src/afi_econ_kit/payouts.py:35 'def apply_gauge(pool, shares)'; payouts.py:130 compute_epoch_payouts(... maturity_hold_frac)
notable_findings.recommendation: Track as the research locus for splits-vs-single-beneficiary mint (TENSION #4). If promoted, extract a normative emissions/splits spec rather than leaving formulas in a research kit.

notable_findings.title: BenchKit merit (reputation/poi/poinsight) blended into allocation weights
notable_findings.evidence: src/afi_econ_kit/gauge.py:120-175 'allocation_gauge(... bench_merit, bench_merit_weights)' and 'raw_allocation[role] *= multipliers[role]'; params/gauge_v0.yaml:16-25 bench_merit_weights mapping
notable_findings.recommendation: Consistent with North Star (reputation influences allocation, not scoring). Keep this boundary explicit if formulas become canonical.

notable_findings.title: Self-contained provenance stamp + determinism tests, not tied to protocol replay spec
notable_findings.evidence: src/afi_econ_kit/schemas.py:97 'class ProvenanceStamp(... git_sha, config_hash, data_hash)'; tests/test_replay_determinism.py:12 test_monte_carlo_determinism; src/afi_econ_kit/cli.py:443 cmd_econ_replay
notable_findings.recommendation: None for research use; reconcile with VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC only if any model is promoted to a protocol-determinism path.

notable_findings.title: 'WHITEPAPER READY' audit claim contradicts placeholder-model warnings
notable_findings.evidence: out_audit/AUDIT.md:29 'WHITEPAPER READY: ... suitable for academic publication.'; README.md:25 'Do NOT use for: ... Whitepaper claims (formulas are not final)'
notable_findings.recommendation: Scope the audit's conclusion to 'harness/invariant integrity', not formula validity, to avoid presenting toy formulas as protocol-canonical.

notable_findings.title: Stale internal naming: package renamed to afi-econ but afi-econ-kit persists everywhere
notable_findings.evidence: pyproject.toml:6 'name = "afi-econ-kit"'; src/index.ts:16 'afi-econ-kit loaded'; Makefile:27 'afi-econ-kit:latest'; src/afi_econ_kit/scenarios.py:394 footer 'afi-econ-kit | git:...'
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag stale; align internal package id/docker tags/footers with the repo's standardized afi-econ name (last commit 570f8b9 standardized references but missed these).

notable_findings.title: Hardcoded developer absolute path committed in audit report
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag stale; regenerate or gitignore out_audit artifacts so machine-specific absolute paths are not committed.

notable_findings.title: package.json test script is a stub while a real pytest suite exists
notable_findings.evidence: package.json:13 '"test": "echo \"(stub - tests not yet implemented)\""' vs ~25 tests under tests/ (e.g. tests/test_replay_determinism.py, test_budget_golden.py)
notable_findings.recommendation: Info/hygiene: the TS package test stub understates the Python test coverage; align scripts if both stacks are maintained.
